{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Representative",
  "description": "Smart Nuel Communications Limited is a growing mobile technology retail company headquartered in Ikeja Computer Village, Lagos, one of Nigeria&rsquo;s largest technology markets. The company specializes in the sales and distribution of mobile phones and related accessories across major cities in the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Sales Representative

Job Locations:&nbsp;Ikeja, and Lekki - Lagos, and Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	This role is responsible for processing daily sales transactions, issuing invoices, maintaining accurate shelf stock records, and providing excellent customer service.
	The role also involves administrative support duties, daily reporting, and ensuring a clean and organized work environment while supporting the branch manager with assigned responsibilities.


Responsibilities


	Receive sales orders from customers, accurately entering them into the system daily.
	Prepare and issue sales invoices promptly and accurately.
	Ensure proper documentation of all sales transactions.
	Conduct stock counts and submit accurate stock reports twice a week.
	Monitor stock levels and report shortages or discrepancies to management.
	Prepare and submit weekly and monthly sales reports.
	Maintain accurate records of sales, inventory, and customer transactions.
	Receive and attend to customer phone calls and complaints professionally.
	Provide customer support and forward complaints or issues to the manager when necessary.
	Maintain positive relationships with customers through courteous and efficient service.
	Ensure cleanliness and organization of the office environment.
	Carry out any other responsibilities assigned by the branch manager.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess an OND, or HND qualification with 2 - 3 years of experience.


Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should their CV to:&nbsp;jobs.smartnuel@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-27",

  },

Sales Representative Job at Smart Nuel Communications Limited




        
    

        
        Smart Nuel Communications Limited is a growing mobile technology retail company headquartered in Ikeja Computer Village, Lagos, one of Nigeria’s largest technology markets. The company specializes in the sales and distribution of mobile phones and related accessories across major cities in the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Representative

Job Locations: Ikeja, and Lekki - Lagos, and Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	This role is responsible for processing daily sales transactions, issuing invoices, maintaining accurate shelf stock records, and providing excellent customer service.
	The role also involves administrative support duties, daily reporting, and ensuring a clean and organized work environment while supporting the branch manager with assigned responsibilities.


Responsibilities


	Receive sales orders from customers, accurately entering them into the system daily.
	Prepare and issue sales invoices promptly and accurately.
	Ensure proper documentation of all sales transactions.
	Conduct stock counts and submit accurate stock reports twice a week.
	Monitor stock levels and report shortages or discrepancies to management.
	Prepare and submit weekly and monthly sales reports.
	Maintain accurate records of sales, inventory, and customer transactions.
	Receive and attend to customer phone calls and complaints professionally.
	Provide customer support and forward complaints or issues to the manager when necessary.
	Maintain positive relationships with customers through courteous and efficient service.
	Ensure cleanliness and organization of the office environment.
	Carry out any other responsibilities assigned by the branch manager.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess an OND, or HND qualification with 2 - 3 years of experience.


Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Personal Assistant",
  "description": "Lington &amp; Bernie Consulting Limited was established on October 8, 1999 to provide efficient and qualitative consulting services to the oil and gas industry as well as banks and financial institutions in the areas of human resources placement and management, human resources consulting services, tax administration and payroll services as well as oil and gas fiscal and contractual terms advisory services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Personal Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos-Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	A Personal Assistant (PA) who provides personalized administrative, technical, or creative support to individuals, often executives or high-net-worth individuals, to enhance productivity.


Key Responsibilities


	Managing busy calendars.
	Screening communication.
	Arranging travel.
	Conducting research, and
	Handling personal errands or household matters.


Core Responsibilities


	Personal Assistance:&nbsp;Running errands, managing household staff, or handling personal projects.
	Liaison &amp; Communication:&nbsp;Acting as the first point of contact between managers and clients, staff, or vendors.
	Calendar &amp; Email Management:&nbsp;Scheduling meetings, appointments, and reminders while controlling access to the manager.
	Travel Coordination:&nbsp;Booking flights, accommodation, and organizing detailed travel itineraries.
	Administrative Support:&nbsp;Handling correspondence, screening phone calls, taking meeting minutes, and preparing reports or presentations.


Required Skills and Qualifications


	Organization:&nbsp;Exceptional time management skills to prioritize tasks.
	Discretion:&nbsp;Ability to handle confidential and sensitive information.
	Communication:&nbsp;Strong verbal and written communication skills.
	Technical Proficiency:&nbsp;Proficiency in office software (e.g., MS Office, Google Workspace).
	Flexibility:&nbsp;Adaptability to changing schedules, which may require working outside standard business hours.


Typical Qualifications:


	While a Degree is not always required, experience in administrative roles, customer service, or secretarial work is highly valued.
	Age: 35-40.
	Gender: Male.


Differences in Roles:


	Corporate PA:&nbsp;Focuses on office tasks, meetings, and business correspondence.
	Private / Personal PA:&nbsp;Manages personal life, including household tasks, shopping, and family schedules.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;jobs@lingtonandbernie.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Personal Assistant Job at Lington & Bernie Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Lington & Bernie Consulting Limited was established on October 8, 1999 to provide efficient and qualitative consulting services to the oil and gas industry as well as banks and financial institutions in the areas of human resources placement and management, human resources consulting services, tax administration and payroll services as well as oil and gas fiscal and contractual terms advisory services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Personal Assistant

Job Location: Lagos-Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	A Personal Assistant (PA) who provides personalized administrative, technical, or creative support to individuals, often executives or high-net-worth individuals, to enhance productivity.


Key Responsibilities


	Managing busy calendars.
	Screening communication.
	Arranging travel.
	Conducting research, and
	Handling personal errands or household matters.


Core Responsibilities


	Personal Assistance: Running errands, managing household staff, or handling personal projects.
	Liaison & Communication: Acting as the first point of contact between managers and clients, staff, or vendors.
	Calendar & Email Management: Scheduling meetings, appointments, and reminders while controlling access to the manager.
	Travel Coordination: Booking flights, accommodation, and organizing detailed travel itineraries.
	Administrative Support: Handling correspondence, screening phone calls, taking meeting minutes, and preparing reports or presentations.


Required Skills and Qualifications


	Organization: Exceptional time management skills to prioritize tasks.
	Discretion: Ability to handle confidential and sensitive information.
	Communication: Strong verbal and written communication skills.
	Technical Proficiency: Proficiency in office software (e.g., MS Office, Google Workspace).
	Flexibility: Adaptability to changing schedules, which may require working outside standard business hours.


Typical Qualifications:


	While a Degree is not always required, experience in administrative roles, customer service, or secretarial work is highly valued.
	Age: 35-40.
	Gender: Male.


Differences in Roles:


	Corporate PA: Focuses on office tasks, meetings, and business correspondence.
	Private / Personal PA: Manages personal life, including household tasks, shopping, and family schedules.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "description": "SAVGRET Consulting Services is a dynamic firm specializing in providing tailored solutions to businesses and organizations across various industries. We strive to deliver exceptional consulting services with a focus on quality, integrity, and client satisfaction. Our mission is to help clients achieve operational excellence through innovative strategies and robust methods. At SAVGRET Consulting Services, we prioritize the professional development of our team members and offer a collaborative, growth-oriented environment.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE Officer
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

Role Description


	This is a full-time, on-site role based in Lagos for a Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) Officer in the Construction Industry.
	The HSE Officer will be responsible for conducting risk assessments, ensuring compliance with safety regulations, and implementing workplace safety measures.
	Daily tasks include guiding construction teams on safe practices, conducting training sessions, inspecting sites for health and safety compliance, and preparing reports to address any occupational health and safety concerns.


Qualifications


	Experience in the construction industry or similar high-risk environments is preferred
	Relevant certification(s) in Health, Safety, and Environment or similar field
	Proficiency in Health &amp; Safety practices and Workplace Safety standards
	Thorough knowledge of Environment Health and Safety (EHS) regulations and policies
	Expertise in Occupational Health and NEBOSH certification is an advantage
	Strong communication skills for effective training and reporting
	Ability to analyze risks, develop preventive measures, and handle safety management systems.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV&nbsp;(resume) to:&nbsp;savgretconsulting@gmail.com&nbsp;/&nbsp;hrbp2@rhemaprojectng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

HSE Officer Job at SAVGRET Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        SAVGRET Consulting Services is a dynamic firm specializing in providing tailored solutions to businesses and organizations across various industries. We strive to deliver exceptional consulting services with a focus on quality, integrity, and client satisfaction. Our mission is to help clients achieve operational excellence through innovative strategies and robust methods. At SAVGRET Consulting Services, we prioritize the professional development of our team members and offer a collaborative, growth-oriented environment.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE Officer
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

Role Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	This is a full-time, on-site role based in Lagos for a Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) Officer in the Construction Industry.
	The HSE Officer will be responsible for conducting risk assessments, ensuring compliance with safety regulations, and implementing workplace safety measures.
	Daily tasks include guiding construction teams on safe practices, conducting training sessions, inspecting sites for health and safety compliance, and preparing reports to address any occupational health and safety concerns.


Qualifications


	Experience in the construction industry or similar high-risk environments is preferred
	Relevant certification(s) in Health, Safety, and Environment or similar field
	Proficiency in Health & Safety practices and Workplace Safety standards
	Thorough knowledge of Environment Health and Safety (EHS) regulations and policies
	Expertise in Occupational Health and NEBOSH certification is an advantage
	Strong communication skills for effective training and reporting
	Ability to analyze risks, develop preventive measures, and handle safety management systems.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV (resume) to: [email protected] / [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

  "description": "GPGATE Solar and Power Nigeria Company Limited&nbsp;is a fast-growing renewable energy company based in Lagos, Nigeria. We specialize in providing reliable solar power solutions to homes, businesses, and industries, helping Nigerians achieve energy independence and reduce reliance on generators. We are passionate about clean energy, innovation, and building a sustainable future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Solar Engineer&nbsp;
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Job Summary


	We are looking for a skilled Solar Engineer to join our expanding team.


Key Responsibilities


	Conduct site surveys, load assessments, and design solar PV systems
	Oversee installation, testing, commissioning, and maintenance of solar systems
	Troubleshoot inverters, batteries, panels, and related equipment
	Ensure projects meet safety, quality, and regulatory standards


Requirements


	B.Eng / B.Sc in Electrical/Electronics Engineering or related field
	2+ years of practical experience in solar PV installation and design
	Valid driver&rsquo;s license and willingness to work on-site.


Note


	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted within 7&ndash;10 working days.
	Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

Solar Engineer Job at GPGATE Solar and Power Nigeria Company Limited




        
    

        
        GPGATE Solar and Power Nigeria Company Limited is a fast-growing renewable energy company based in Lagos, Nigeria. We specialize in providing reliable solar power solutions to homes, businesses, and industries, helping Nigerians achieve energy independence and reduce reliance on generators. We are passionate about clean energy, innovation, and building a sustainable future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Solar Engineer 
Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	We are looking for a skilled Solar Engineer to join our expanding team.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Conduct site surveys, load assessments, and design solar PV systems
	Oversee installation, testing, commissioning, and maintenance of solar systems
	Troubleshoot inverters, batteries, panels, and related equipment
	Ensure projects meet safety, quality, and regulatory standards


Requirements


	B.Eng / B.Sc in Electrical/Electronics Engineering or related field
	2+ years of practical experience in solar PV installation and design
	Valid driver’s license and willingness to work on-site.


Note


	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted within 7–10 working days.
	Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis.
    

    

        Click here to Apply
